mainText:
Good morning. Here are your headlines for Thursday 28 May 2026.

AI models and products. Densest release cluster of 2026 between May 13 and May 23 shipped ten-plus launches with Gemini 3.5 Flash, Composer 2.5, Grok Build, and Gemini Omni.

UK personal finance. Best easy-access ISA rates reach 4.62% as two-year fixed ISAs hit 4.72%.

Israeli politics. 110 lawmakers backed parliament dissolution bill on May 20.

UK politics. Labour lost 1,100 council seats while Reform UK gained over 1,400 in local elections.

Sleep and wearables. Google announced the Fitbit Air on May 7 and reviewers received units May 24, disrupting the screenless tracker market at $99.

B2B SaaS and go-to-market. AI-enhanced SaaS startups raise at 30-40 percent higher valuations than non-AI competitors.

Today's question: With Series A rounds now requiring eighteen million dollars in median size and NRR compressing to one hundred and one percent, are you positioning your GTM advisory clients to hit those expansion efficiency thresholds before their next funding round, and if not, should you be building a fractional RevOps service to own that gap?
fullText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Thursday 28 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products
- Gemini 3.5 Flash launched as general availability with frontier-level intelligence at 4x speed of comparable models at $1.50/$9 per 1 million tokens with 1M context window.
- Subquadratic launched SubQ 1M-Preview on May 5 with $29 million in seed funding as the first major commercial challenge to the Transformer architecture, shipping with native 12 million token context window and roughly 1/5 the cost of frontier models on long-context tasks.
- Zyphra released ZAYA1-8B under Apache 2.0 on May 6 with Mixture-of-Experts routing activating only 760 million parameters per token, and was trained end to end on AMD Instinct hardware.

💷 UK personal finance
- Trading 212 leads easy-access Cash ISAs at 4.76% for new money, with Moneybox at 4.75% for transfers.
- Mortgage rates have started to fall in May 2026 with major lenders including Nationwide, HSBC, Halifax and Santander cutting rates, though the Bank of England held the base rate at 3.75% on 30 April and pointed to the likelihood of higher interest rates.
- From April 2027, under-65s will only be able to contribute 12,000 pounds per year to a Cash ISA down from 20,000 pounds, making 2026 an important year to maximise Cash ISA contributions before the new limits take effect.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics
- On May 12, Dov Lando of Degel HaTorah called for Knesset dissolution after Netanyahu lacked votes to pass Haredi military exemption law.
- Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid announced joint alliance "Together" on April 26 for October elections, challenging Netanyahu's Likud which leads polls but whose coalition trails.
- Netanyahu stated Israel will not withdraw from 53 percent of Gaza or allow reconstruction until Hamas disarms, directly contradicting Trump's January 2026 Gaza peace charter framework.

🇬🇧 UK politics
- Around eighty Labour MPs have called on Starmer to set a date for his departure as of 11 May 2026, with Andy Burnham, the mayor of Manchester, shown in polls as the most popular politician in Britain and potential successor if Starmer steps down.
- Reform UK took control of Havering, its first London local authority, alongside Essex county council and Sunderland, with 14 councils gained in the breakthrough local election results.
- 200 million pounds in new funding backs a teacher training programme for special educational needs under the schools white paper, while the government expects every secondary school eventually to have an inclusion base.

😴 Sleep and wearables
- Fitbit Air starts at $99.99 and works without a paid subscription for basic health tracking, while reviewers noted the Fitbit Air got remarkably close to the much pricier Oura Ring 4 in direct sleep comparison tests.
- Oura Gen 3 and Gen 4 demonstrated the highest accuracy for resting heart rate and HRV against ECG reference, while WHOOP showed moderate agreement across 536 nights of validation data.
- Oura Ring was 5 percent more accurate than Apple Watch and 10 percent more accurate than Fitbit in four-stage sleep classification according to a 2024 Brigham and Women's Hospital study.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market
- Series A median round sizes increased from 12 million to 18 million dollars, reflecting stricter traction requirements before institutional investment arrives.
- Fintech company Gupshup deployed 11x's Alice AI sales agent and saw a 50 percent increase in Sales Qualified Leads per SDR, making each human rep 1.5 times more productive.
- Median Net Revenue Retention compressed to 101 percent in 2026, while top performers maintain 111 percent or higher, making expansion efficiency the new differentiator among SaaS companies.

Today's question: With Series A rounds now requiring eighteen million dollars in median size and NRR compressing to one hundred and one percent, are you positioning your GTM advisory clients to hit those expansion efficiency thresholds before their next funding round, and if not, should you be building a fractional RevOps service to own that gap?
